scope: FQ-11: the workbench/furnace/anvil station chain that consumes the FQ-10 ores - buildable stations, smelting to ingots, bronze alloying, and anvil metal gear, with the raw-ore -> ingot -> gear gate. No farming, world-gen, or block placement.
summary: The FQ-10 ores now matter. Three buildable stations form a tech chain in data/recipes.json 'stations': workbench (no prereq), furnace (needs workbench), anvil (needs furnace), each with a build_cost paid from the Town Hall stockpile. build_state is settlement-owned (stations_built) and saved in the Town Hall's to_dict/from_dict, with pre-FQ-11 saves defaulting to nothing built. A single town_hall.craft_station handles every station recipe: it pulls inputs from the stockpile and routes outputs by recipe - smelted ingots (output_to: stockpile) remain in the stockpile, anvil gear (equip_slots) equips onto the player, and anything else goes to inventory. Critically, the gear empty-slot + fit checks run BEFORE inputs are consumed, mirroring the FQ-04 forge guards so a full slot or a data regression can never eat the stockpile. The metallurgy: the furnace smelts raw ore + coal into copper/tin/iron/silver ingots and alloys bronze from copper + tin ingots; the anvil forges iron gear (sword_iron at attack 5, and an iron helm/cuirass/boots set) from ingots. The metal gate is structural and enforced by both the validator and the smoke: no recipe converts raw ore straight to gear - you smelt first, then forge. Crude wood/stone gear stays exactly as it was; iron is the anvil-gated upgrade. Supporting data: the new ores and coal became depositable, ingots are new items.json entries, iron gear is new in equipment.json, and the workbench hosts a basic wood+coal torch-bundle recipe. The Town Hall panel gained a data-driven, scrollable station section (build buttons annotated when a prerequisite is missing or the stockpile is short, and per-station recipe buttons that disable when unaffordable or, for gear, when the slot is filled), wired through game_root. Suite 262 -> 269.
acceptance: Station recipes locked until built, and no station builds before its prerequisite (fq11_station_gating)
Workbench -> furnace build chain spends its build costs from the stockpile (fq11_build_chain)
Furnace smelts raw ore + coal into an ingot placed in the stockpile, not inventory (fq11_furnace_smelts_ore)
Anvil forges the iron sword from ingots; equipped, attack 5 (fq11_anvil_forges_iron_gear)
Metal gate: with only raw ore and no ingots the anvil cannot forge the sword (fq11_metal_gate_no_ore_shortcut)
Bronze alloy from copper + tin ingots at the furnace (fq11_bronze_alloy)
Suite 269/269; validator (prereq chain + metal gate) and capsule doctor green
validation: python scripts/validate_repo.py -> PASS (exit 0), incl. 'station chain and metal gate'
COHERONIA_SMOKE=1 waited headless run -> 269/269 PASS, 7 new fq11_* checks green
git diff --check -> 0 whitespace errors (LF->CRLF notices only)
decisions: Stations are Town-Hall-panel-built settlement upgrades (state saved), not placeable world blocks - the bounded reading of 'tie station presence to Town Hall/village UI without complex NPCs'
One unified craft_station with output routing (stockpile ingots / equipped gear / inventory) rather than a special function per recipe, with gear guards consuming nothing on failure
Metal gate made structural: no ore->gear recipe exists; the validator forbids raw ore in anvil inputs and the smoke proves ore alone can't forge
Crude wood/stone gear left untouched; iron is the strictly-gated upgrade (crude town_hall -> iron anvil)
Tech-tree prereqs (furnace needs workbench, anvil needs furnace + iron_ingot build cost) give the chain shape and gate the anvil behind actual smelting
Self-review only (no agent spawned) for a change fully covered by the validator + 7 smoke checks
risks: The station section is below the forge buttons in a scrollable panel, so build/craft buttons can be below the fold; a future UI pass could tab/reorganize. Panel renders without breakage but the shots tour captures it unscrolled
Ingot economy and iron-gear balance are data-tunable and untested by human play
Ingots and built stations are settlement/save state only; no world-gen or placement change
next_action: FQ-12: farming and food stability (plantable/regrowable crops on the requires_support groundwork).
